name: Text POP
description: The Text POP creates geometry from any [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TrueType TrueType] or [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenType OpenType] font that is installed on the system, or any TrueType/OpenType font file on disk. [[Unicode]] is supported. Text POP supports two connectivity modes Triangles, and Line Strips. Extrusion is enabled for Triangle mode. Line Strips create a closed line strip for each polygon. When Bridge Holes is toggled, if a glyph has holes these polygons will be bridged together to form a single closed line strip. '''Process multiple strings transformed separately''': The Specification DAT and Specification CHOP allow for individual strings to be each positioned and styled independently in 3D. Each line in the specification OP is an indepedent text block with its own alignment. Every row of the Spec DAT represents a "text block". Every Spec DAT contains a column named text which is the string that overrides the Text parameter. Most columns override a Text POP parameter, such as text, tx and fontsize. Some column names noted below have no corresponding parameter. The Spec CHOP can also specify values per block. When including a Spec CHOP the number of Spec CHOP samples needs to be the same number of rows as the Spec DAT following the first column header. Parameters that can be overridden are tx, ty, tz, rx, ry, rz, sx, sy, sz, px, py, pz, tracking, trackingx, trackingy, linespacing, wordwrap, wordwrapsize, fontsize, fontsizex, fontsizey, fontcolorr, fontcolorg, fontcolorb, fontcolora, text, alignx and aligny. tracking and fontsize are the same as trackingx and fontsizex respectively. text, alignx and aligny can only be specified through the Table DAT. To put newlines or tabs into the text string formatting, turn the Text parameter into expression mode and use \n and \t respectively. For example 'first\nsecond' will put the two words on separate lines. '''Transforming text blocks''': The column or channel names can include tx, ty, tz and all the rotate and scale channels (which use the Transform Order parameter). A special column/channel called append can be used, when append is 1, to position one block of text relative to the end of the previous block. Regular transforms are inherited by each appended text block. Local transforms (ltx, lrz, etc), can be used to apply additional transforms to a block that are not inherited by appended blocks. If the special column/channel localxform is set to 'pre', then the local transform is applied in world space before the inherited transforms are applied. '''Text POP vs Geo Text COMP vs Text COMP vs Text TOP''' - The Text POP creates linestrip outlines and triangles, existing as 3D data that can be further manipulated in POPs. The Geo Text COMP, when rendered with a Render TOP, creates pixels without converting to POPs or a 3D outline form - they are rendered directly on quadrilateral polygons in the 3D scene as if they are texture maps. The Text COMP does something similar in 2D panels - strings are rendered directly into panel pixels, and the Text COMP is interactive like any panel COMP. The Text TOP simply renders the pixels directly into a TOP image in the same way. Note: The Text POP does most of its work on the CPU. See also: [[Text TOP]], [[Text COMP]], [[Geo Text COMP]], [[Unicode]], [[Triangulate POP]], [[Extrude POP]].

parameters.name: Connectivity
parameters.label: Connectivity
parameters.description: Determines the primitive used to connect the points.

parameters.name: Bridge Holes
parameters.label: Bridge Holes
parameters.description: Bridges holes between interior and exterior line strips for glyphs with holes. Only available for Line Strips.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.label: Mode
parameters.description: Controls whether the contents are taken from the Text parameter or from a Specification DAT/CHOP.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.label: Text
parameters.description: The string of text to create as geometry. If newlines or tabs are desired, change this parameter to expression mode, and specify a Python string that includes \n or \t to signify newlines and tabs. E.g 'First Line\nSecond Line'.

parameters.name: Specification DAT
parameters.label: Specification DAT
parameters.description: A path to a [[Table DAT]] that is the source of the text data when in Specification mode. The table should have 2 or 3 columns labeled tx, ty, tx and text in its first row. Each row after that will display one line of text at the given x, y coordinates as measured from the bottom-left corner. x and y are measured in POP space units.

parameters.name: Specification CHOP
parameters.label: Specification CHOP
parameters.description: A path to a CHOP that can provide numerical data when in Specification mode. Channels named tx, ty or tz can be used to position the text data specified in the Specificaction DAT. Each data row in the DAT should have a corresponding sample in the CHOP.

parameters.name: Scale Font to BBox Height
parameters.label: Scale Font to BBox Height
parameters.description: Scale the font’s vertical size so it’s based on the vertical bounding box of the font.

parameters.name: Break Language
parameters.label: Break Language
parameters.description: Language type hint to help format the glyphs correctly. This should be a abbreviation from the Text TOP/SOP Unicode Language Abbreviations table.

parameters.name: Reading Direction
parameters.label: Reading Direction
parameters.description: Use to set whether the language reads Left to Right or Right to Left.

parameters.name: Word Wrap
parameters.label: Word Wrap
parameters.description: When checked text is automatically line wrapped once it takes up the space set in Word Wrap Size parameter below.

parameters.name: Word Wrap Size
parameters.label: Word Wrap Size
parameters.description: Determines the amount of 3D space used before the line wraps.

parameters.name: Smart Punctuation
parameters.label: Smart Punctuation
parameters.description: Enables the use of smart punctuation. For example, pairs of quotes will be replaced with appropriate angled quotes, 3 periods will be replaced with an ellipses character and two hypens will become an em-dash.

parameters.name: Level of Detail
parameters.label: Level of Detail
parameters.description: Controls the quality of the text's shape by adding/removing subdivisions to the geometry.

parameters.name: Font
parameters.label: Font
parameters.description: Select the font for the text from this drop down menu. All fonts are provided by the OS, any [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TrueType TrueType] font that is loaded into the OS can be used.

parameters.name: Font File
parameters.label: Font File
parameters.description: Specify any TrueType or OpenType font file (.ttf, .otf file) to use for the text. When using a font file, the Font menu above is disabled.

parameters.name: Typeface
parameters.label: Typeface
parameters.description: Select a specific typeface for the current font e.g. Bold, Regular, Italic, etc.

parameters.name: Font Size
parameters.label: Font Size
parameters.description: Sets the font size. The font size defines the distance from the baseline to the top of the layout box for the given font. The default size of 1 of the default font is close to the vertical size of capital letters with no descenders.

parameters.name: Keep Font Ratio
parameters.label: Keep Font Ratio
parameters.description: Ignores Y value in Font Size. Sets both X and Y size to Font Size X.

parameters.name: Tracking
parameters.label: Tracking
parameters.description: The amount of space to add between letters in X and Y. Tracking is way of adding an arbitrary offset between letters. There already is a default offset associated with each font so the letters are flush against each other. The Tracking parameter this adds to that and allows for a Y offset.

parameters.name: Line Spacing
parameters.label: Line Spacing
parameters.description: Determines the amount of space between lines of text.

parameters.name: Horizontal Align
parameters.label: Horizontal Align
parameters.description: Determines how the text is positioned horizontally in the panel. For example, with left alignment, the text will be positioned against the left side of the panel plus any defined padding.

parameters.name: Vertical Align
parameters.label: Vertical Align
parameters.description: Determines how the text is positioned vertically inside the panel.

parameters.name: Font Color
parameters.label: Font Color
parameters.description: The default color of the text.

parameters.name: Font Alpha
parameters.label: Font Alpha
parameters.description: The blending value used to display the text. 1 is opaque while 0 is transparent.

parameters.name: Extrude
parameters.label: Extrude
parameters.description: Extrude each glyph. Only enabled when set to Triangles.

parameters.name: Extrude Depth
parameters.label: Extrude Depth
parameters.description: Extrusion depth.

parameters.name: Transform Order
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: Sets the overall transform order for the transformations. The transform order determines the order in which transformations take place. Depending on the order, you can achieve different results using the exact same values. Choose the appropriate order from the menu.

parameters.name: Rotate Order
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description: Sets the order of the rotations within the overall transform order.

parameters.name: Translate
parameters.label: Translate
parameters.description: These three fields move the geometry in the three axes.

parameters.name: Rotate
parameters.label: Rotate
parameters.description: These three fields rotate the geometry in the three axes.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description: These three fields scale the geometry in the three axes.

parameters.name: Pivot
parameters.label: Pivot
parameters.description: The pivot point for the transformations (not the same as the pivot point in the pivot channels). The pivot point parameters allow you to define the point about which geometry scales and rotates. Altering the pivot point produces different results depending on the transformation performed on the object. For example, during a scaling operation, if the pivot point of an object is located at: -1, -1, 0 and you wanted to scale the object by 0.5 (reduce its size by 50%) the object would scale toward the pivot point and appear to slide down and to the left.

_provenance.fact: scraped from the live Text_POP wiki page
